_meta.notes: Five beats for a dragon-egg run in the Forsaken Village. The egg sits in a banked hearth in the smithy. The smith Berra fled with everyone else mid-stroke; the fire is dying. The arc is to keep heartbeat alive long enough to be found by someone — anyone — who recognizes what an egg this size means. Win: heartbeat>=6 at turn>=8 AND a sympathetic NPC introduced. Lose: warmth==0 from the cold getting in. Multiple sub-beats track who comes back.

beats.narrative: The smithy is warm in patches and cold in others. The hearth is banked, just barely. Berra was here — her hammer is on the anvil, mid-stroke — but she is not here now. Whatever called the village left her in a hurry.

suggestedVerbs.label: absorb the hearth's warmth
suggestedVerbs.description: the embers' last heat into your shell

suggestedVerbs.label: listen to the empty smithy
suggestedVerbs.description: the world arrives as pressures and rhythms

suggestedVerbs.label: hum low
suggestedVerbs.description: the room takes on the warmth of breath

beats.narrative: A draft finds the smithy through a crack in the door. It is colder than the day outside. The colder cold is the cold of a place you do not want to be from.

suggestedVerbs.label: pulse warmth
suggestedVerbs.description: out from your core to your shell and back

suggestedVerbs.label: draw the hearth deeper
suggestedVerbs.description: take what reserve you can from the embers

fires.detail: a draft from the western road carrying air older than the day

beats.narrative: Footsteps on the village square. A single set, careful — not Berra's, but they smell of leather and an oil you almost recognize. Whoever it is paused, then turned toward the smithy.

suggestedVerbs.label: ring the shell
suggestedVerbs.description: a note older than the dam who keeps you

suggestedVerbs.label: hum low
suggestedVerbs.description: let them notice you are awake

suggestedVerbs.label: kindle the glow brighter
suggestedVerbs.description: warm the room enough that they enter

beats.narrative: The finder stands over the hearth. Their breath is shallow. They have understood what you are. The next move is theirs.

suggestedVerbs.label: show them what you are
suggestedVerbs.description: a memory not yours, of a long ridge and a thermal that did not hold

suggestedVerbs.label: dream a memory
suggestedVerbs.description: let them see a name you do not yet know

suggestedVerbs.label: hum, just for them
suggestedVerbs.description: a register meant for one heart at a time

beats.narrative: The finder kneels. They place a hand-warm of something close to the shell — not enough to stir you, just enough to keep what you have. The cold loses its appetite. You are not yet alone in this world.

suggestedVerbs.label: return the warmth
suggestedVerbs.description: out from your core to their hand and back; the trust completes itself

suggestedVerbs.label: show them the long ridge
suggestedVerbs.description: a memory not yours, shared at the moment of being kept

suggestedVerbs.label: ring the shell for them
suggestedVerbs.description: a note older than the dam who keeps you, given to the one who chose to stay
